buttons.notAccessTitle: You don't have permission to access
buttons.columnsTooltip: Choose, reorder and hide table columns

warnWhenUnsavedChanges: Are you sure you want to leave? You have unsaved changes.

notifications.createError: There was an error creating {{resource}} (status code: {{statusCode}})

errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong
errorBoundary.subTitle: This page failed to render. If it started after changing or sharing view settings, resetting them usually fixes it.

apiToken.title: API token required
apiToken.help: This Spoolman instance requires an API token. Enter it to continue; it is stored in this browser.

users.title: User accounts
users.help: Create accounts to require login. Read-only users can view everything but not make changes. The first account is always an administrator.
users.reset_password_help: Enter a new password for this user.

colorFilter.title: Filter by similar colour
colorFilter.help: Show items whose colour is within this tolerance of the picked colour.

generic.title: Printing
generic.description: Tune in the settings below to get the desired print layout. Keep in mind that printers and your OS may apply it's own margins and scaling, so you may need to perform some trial-and-error before it's all correct. Test it on a piece of regular paper before printing on the actual labels.
generic.helpMargin: Margins should be configured to match your label paper and printer, changing these will affect the size of the entire grid.
generic.helpPrinterMargin: Safe-Zone should be set to how close to the paper edge your printer can print, changing these will not affect the entire grid.

pageSizeMode.label: Page Size
pageSizeMode.tooltip: "Automatic" lets the printer/browser choose the page geometry (default). "Match label size" emits an explicit @page size from the paper dimensions above — use this for roll/label printers (e.g. Brother QL) so labels print at the right size and orientation. Best supported in Chrome.

generic.deleteSettingsConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this preset?
generic.invalidGeometry: The label grid does not fit on the paper
generic.invalidGeometryHelp: Margins and spacing leave no room for the labels. Reduce the margins, spacing, columns or rows — or pick a larger paper size.
generic.contentClipped: Some label content is cut off
generic.contentClippedHelp: At least one label’s content does not fit inside its label and will be clipped when printed. Reduce the text size, shorten the template, or use fewer columns/rows so each label is larger.

qrcode.title: Label Printing
qrcode.templateHelp: Use {} to insert values of the spool object as text. For example, {id} will be replaced with the spool id, or {filament.material} will be replaced with the material of the spool. if a value is missing it will be replaced with "?". A second set of {} can be used to remove this. In addition, any text between the sets of {} will be removed if the value is missing. For example, {Lot Nr: {lot_nr}} will only show the label if the spool has a lot number. Enclose text with double asterix ** to make it bold. Add a format after a colon: numbers like {remaining_weight:0.0} round to that many decimals, and dates like {registered:YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm} format the date/time in your local time zone. Start a line with {size:1.5} to scale that whole line (here 1.5×). Click the button to view a list of all available tags.

textColumns.label: Text Columns
textColumns.tooltip: Lay the label text out across this many columns. Handy for narrow labels with several short lines.

customPayload.label: Custom QR Payload
customPayload.tooltip: Override the scanned QR content with a template (same tags as the label). Leave blank to use the default Spoolman payload. A custom value only scans inside Spoolman if it still matches a recognised format (WEB+SPOOLMAN:S-{id} / F-{id} or a /spool/show/{id} URL).

qrErrorLevel.label: QR Error Correction
qrErrorLevel.tooltip: Higher levels tolerate more damage/dirt on the label but make the QR code denser (harder to scan when small). H (highest) is the default.

qrPlacement.label: QR Placement
qrPlacement.tooltip: Where the QR code sits relative to the label text. Stacking it above or below suits tall, narrow labels.

showColorSwatch.label: Print Colour Swatch
showColorSwatch.tooltip: Print a small coloured box of the filament colour on the label.

barcode1d.label: 1D Barcode
barcode1d.tooltip: Optionally print a 1D barcode of the QR payload alongside the QR code, for scanners that only read linear barcodes.

useHTTPUrl.label: QR code link
useHTTPUrl.tooltip: Will use proprietary link that will work only if scanned from Spoolman's scanning feature (default). URL uses either the base URL specified in settings, or the current page URL if not set.

qrcode.templateHelpFilament: Use {} to insert values of the filament object as text. For example, {id} will be replaced with the filament id, or {vendor.name} will be replaced with the vendor name. If a value is missing it will be replaced with "?". A second set of {} can be used to remove this. In addition, any text between the sets of {} will be removed if the value is missing. For example, {Article: {article_number}} will only show the label if a filament has an article number. Enclose text with double asterix ** to make it bold. Click the button to view a list of all available tags.
qrcode.tooltip: Print labels for the selected rows, or pick items on the next page

spoolSelect.title: Select Spools
spoolSelect.description: Select spools to print labels for.
spoolSelect.reprintConfirmContent_one: {{count}} selected spool already has a printed label. Print it again?
spoolSelect.reprintConfirmContent_other: {{count}} selected spools already have printed labels. Print them again?
spoolSelect.noSpoolsSelected: You have not selected any spools.

filamentSelect.title: Select Filaments
filamentSelect.description: Select filaments to print labels for.
filamentSelect.reprintConfirmContent_one: {{count}} selected filament already has a printed label. Print it again?
filamentSelect.reprintConfirmContent_other: {{count}} selected filaments already have printed labels. Print them again?
filamentSelect.noFilamentsSelected: You have not selected any filaments.

scan.title: Scan
scan.nfc_hint: NFC scanning is not available in this browser. Use Android Chrome, or connect a server-side NFC reader.

move.scan_location: Spool #{{id}} selected. Now scan the destination location.
move.load_error: Failed to load the scanned location.

clear.recognized: Clear-spool code recognised. Spoolman has no active spool; your printer or Moonraker integration handles clearing it.

barcode.unknown_content: No filament has the barcode {{code}}. Create a filament and remember this barcode?
barcode.lookup_error: Could not look up that barcode.

scanner.title: QR Code Scanner
scanner.description: Scan a Spoolman QR code to view details about the spool.

error.notAllowed: You have not allowed access to the camera.
error.insecureContext: The page is not served over HTTPS.
error.streamApiNotSupported: The browser does not support the MediaStream API.

bulk.edit_help: Tick a field to change it on every selected spool. Unticked fields are left unchanged.
bulk.nothing_selected: Tick at least one field to change.

weigh.title: Weigh Spools
weigh.help: Find a spool, enter its measurement, then Save & Next to weigh the next one without closing.
weigh.search_placeholder: Search by ID, filament, lot number…
weigh.tooltip: Re-weigh several spools in a row

fields_help.price: Price of a full spool. If not set, the price of the filament will be assumed instead.
fields_help.weight_to_use: Select what weight value to enter. Measured weight is only available if the spool weight is set for the selected filament.
fields_help.used_weight: How much filament has been used from the spool. A new spool should have 0g used.
fields_help.remaining_weight: How much filament is left on the spool. For a new spool this should match the spool weight.
fields_help.measured_weight: How much the filament and spool weigh.
fields_help.initial_weight: The initial weight of filament on the spool (net weight). Will use the weight from the filament object if not set.
fields_help.spool_weight: The weight of the spool when it is empty. Leave empty to use the value from the filament or the manufacturer.
fields_help.location: Where the spool is located if you have multiple locations where you store your spools.
fields_help.printer: The printer this spool is currently loaded on. Manage your printers in Settings.
fields_help.lot_nr: Manufacturer's lot number. Can be used to ensure a print has consistent colour if multiple spools are used.
fields_help.diameter: Measured filament diameter for this specific spool, used in length calculations. Leave empty to use the filament's diameter.
fields_help.color_hex: Optional colour for this specific spool, overriding the filament's colour. Clear to use the filament's colour.
fields_help.external_filament: You have selected a filament from the external database. A filament object (and possibly a manufacturer object) will be created automatically when you create this spool. This can create duplicate filament objects if you have already created a filament object for this filament.

form.adjust_filament_help: Here you can directly add or subtract filament from the spool. A positive value will consume filament, a negative value will add it.
form.spool_updated: This spool has been updated by someone/something else since you opened this page. Saving will overwrite those changes!

messages.archive: Are you sure you want to archive this spool?
messages.update_error: Could not save changes. Please try again.
messages.reset_usage: Reset this spool's used weight to zero? This clears the first/last-used dates.

sibling_spools.title: Other spools of this filament
sibling_spools.tooltip: List every spool of this filament

weight_history.title: Weight history
weight_history.idle_gain: Measured weight rose by {{grams}} g between measurements — the spool may have absorbed moisture. (Experimental)

fields_help.image: A photo to recognise this filament by — e.g. the spool or the shop listing. Downscaled in the browser before upload; metadata such as GPS location is stripped.
fields_help.name: Filament name, to distinguish this filament type among others from the same manufacturer. Should contain the colour for example.
fields_help.weight: The filament weight of a full spool (net weight). This should not include the weight of the spool itself, only the filament. It is what is usually written on the packaging.
fields_help.spool_weight: The weight of an empty spool. Used to determine measured weight of a spool.
fields_help.low_stock_threshold: Flag this filament for reorder when the total remaining weight across its spools drops below this many grams.
fields_help.reserve_count: Number of unopened spare spools you keep on hand, tracked without creating a spool for each one.
fields_help.multi_color_direction: Filaments can have multiple colours in two ways: either through coextrusion, like dual-colour filaments with consistent multi-colours, or through longitudinal colour changes, like gradient filaments that shift colours along the spool.
fields_help.settings_extruder_temp_range: Manufacturer's recommended extruder temperature range (min–max). Optional, leave blank if you only have a single value.
fields_help.settings_bed_temp_range: Manufacturer's recommended bed temperature range (min–max). Optional, leave blank if you only have a single value.

bulk.edit_help: Tick a field to change it on every selected filament. Unticked fields are left unchanged.
bulk.nothing_selected: Tick at least one field to change.

form.filament_updated: This filament has been updated by someone/something else since you opened this page. Saving will overwrite those changes!
form.import_external_description:
Select a filament in the list to automatically populate its details in the filament creation form. This will overwrite any data you have entered in the form.

A Manufacturer object will be created automatically when you click Ok, if necessary.
form.import_3dfp_help: Automatically fill information by retrieving from 3dfilamentprofiles.com
form.import_3dfp_error: Failed to fetch filament data from 3dfilamentprofiles.com

image.prepare_error: Could not read that image file

swatch.title: 3D-printable swatch
swatch.description: Download a {{width}} × {{height}} mm printable sample card carrying this filament's label info. The card body is printed in the filament itself; the text and QR code form a raised one-layer marking in {{color}}, picked for contrast against the filament colour.
swatch.print_hint: Print the whole card in this filament with a filament change to {{color}} at the last layer, or import the embedded colours in a multi-colour slicer (e.g. Bambu Studio). The QR code stays scannable either way.
swatch.truncated_warning: Some text was shortened to fit on the card.

messages.update_error: Could not save changes. Please try again.

fields_help.empty_spool_weight: The weight of an empty spool from this manufacturer.

form.vendor_updated: This manufacturer has been updated by someone/something else since you opened this page. Saving will overwrite those changes!
form.duplicate_name_warning: A manufacturer with this name already exists.

home.description: It looks like you haven't added any spools yet. See the <helpPageLink>Help page</helpPageLink> for help getting started.
home.shopping_list_empty: No filaments below their low-stock threshold.

resources.filament: Brands of filament. They have properties such as name, material, colour, diameter, and more.
resources.spool: Individual physical spools of a specific filament.
resources.vendor: The companies that make the filament.

help.description:
Here are some tips to get you started.

Spoolman holds 3 different types of data:

To add a new spool to the database, start by creating a Filament object. Once that's done, proceed to create a Spool object for that specific spool. If you acquire additional spools of the same filament later on, simply generate additional Spool objects and reuse the same Filament object.

Optionally, you can also generate a Manufacturer object for the company manufacturing the filament if you wish to track that information.

You have the option to link other 3D printer services to Spoolman, like Moonraker, which can automatically monitor filament usage and update the Spool objects for you. Refer to the Spoolman README for guidance on how to set that up.

base_url.label: Base URL
base_url.tooltip: The base URL to use when generating features such as QR codes.

round_prices.label: Round prices
round_prices.tooltip: Round prices to the nearest whole number.

unit_scaling.label: Scale large units
unit_scaling.tooltip: Display large weights and lengths auto-scaled (e.g. 1.5 kg, 64.37 m) instead of raw grams/millimeters in the list and detail views. Stored values and the API are unaffected.

default_style.label: Default swatch style
default_style.tooltip: The style preselected when downloading a 3D-printable filament swatch. New styles can be contributed to Spoolman in client/src/utils/swatch/styles.ts.

swatch.preview_description: Preview with two example filaments — the text and QR code are automatically printed in black or white, whichever contrasts best with the filament colour:

import_export.export_help: Download your vendors, filaments or spools as CSV or JSON. Vendor and filament data is included with spools.
import_export.import_help: Upload a CSV or JSON file in the same shape as an export. The whole file is applied as one all-or-nothing transaction; use a dry run first to preview the result.
import_export.report_help: Open a printable summary of your current stock.

threemf.title: Import a sliced 3MF project
threemf.help: Upload a sliced .3mf from Bambu Studio or OrcaSlicer to read how much of each filament the print used, match it to your spools, and subtract the usage in one step.
threemf.no_filaments: No filament usage was found in this 3MF.

printers.description: Manage your printers here. Once you have at least one, you can assign a spool to a printer from the spool's create or edit form.
printers.delete_confirm: Delete printer "{{name}}"? Spools assigned to it will be unassigned, not deleted.

custom_links.sidebar_description: Add your own links to the left sidebar, e.g. to your printer's Mainsail or Fluidd page. They open in a new tab.
custom_links.spool_description: Add custom action buttons to each spool. The URL can include the spool's fields in curly braces — {id}, {filament_id}, {location}, {lot_nr}, {comment} — which are filled in per spool, so tools like nfc2klipper or Moonraker's active-spool endpoint are one click away.
custom_links.name: Name
custom_links.url_template_help: Use {id} where the spool ID should go, e.g. http://moonraker.local/...?id={id}

extra_fields.description:
Here you can add extra custom fields to your entities.

Once a field is added, you can not change its key or type, and for choice type fields you can not remove choices or change the multi choice state. If you remove a field, the associated data for all entities will be deleted.

The key is what other programs read/write the data as, so if your custom field is supposed to integrate with a third-party program, make sure to set it correctly. Default value is only applied to new items.

Extra fields can be shown and sorted in the table views. Choice and boolean fields expose filter options there as well, and all extra field types support filtering for empty values.

For spool fields, enable "Copy from Filament" to have a new spool inherit the value from a filament field with the same key at creation time (unless the spool sets its own).

A "Link" field stores a short value per item (e.g. an article number) and turns it into a clickable link using the Link URL template — put {} where the value goes, e.g. https://www.amazon.com/dp/{}.
extra_fields.choices_missing_error: You cannot remove choices. The following choices are missing: {{choices}}
extra_fields.key_not_changed: Please change the key to something else.
extra_fields.delete_confirm_description: This will delete the field and all associated data for all entities.

locations.no_locations_help: This page lets you organise your spools in locations, add some spools to get started!

show.name: Name
show.no_spools: No spools are stored at this location.

print.title: Print Location Labels
print.description: Select the locations to print scannable QR labels for.

calibration.title: Calibration

step_form.recommended_hint: Values here appear as recommended settings on the spool page.

calibration.delete_session_confirm: Delete this calibration session and all its step results?

wizard.title: Calibration Wizard
wizard.subtitle: Follow the OrcaSlicer calibration guide, step by step. You can skip any step and resume later.
wizard.skip_finish_confirm: Mark this session as complete without saving this step?

nfc.scan_description: Scan an NFC tag to view details about the spool.
nfc.no_match: No matching spool found for this tag.
nfc.create_from_tag_description: No matching spool found. You can create a new spool from the tag data below.
nfc.create_error: Failed to create spool from tag data.
nfc.unsupported: NFC is not supported in this browser.
nfc.browser_ndef_warning: Warning: Browser NFC writes use NDEF format. Tags written this way will NOT be recognised by the TigerTag app, which expects raw binary on pages 4-39. Use Server mode for TigerTag-compatible writes, or Download Raw Binary for use with an external NFC tool.
nfc.browser_write_success: TigerTag data written to NFC tag via NDEF.

error.not_supported: Web NFC is not supported in this browser. Use the Server mode with a connected reader.

nfc.bind_description: Scan an existing NFC tag to link it to this spool. Future scans of this tag will automatically select this spool.
nfc.bind_confirm_description: Tag scanned. Review the tag data below and confirm to link it to this spool.
nfc.bind_error: Failed to link tag to spool.
nfc.bind_no_tigertag: No TigerTag data found on this tag. Only TigerTag format is supported for linking.
nfc.qidi_write_info: Qidi tags store only material and colour. The tag will be written with the closest matching Qidi material and colour codes.
